guide.title: Getting started
guide.subtitle: From joining a team to tracking your spend — everything you need for your first call.
guide.step1Title: Join a team & issue an API key
guide.step1Body: All usage and budgets are managed per team. Join a team first, then create an API key that belongs to it.
guide.step1Item1: Find your team in Discover Teams and request to join.
guide.step1Item2: Once approved, create a key from My Keys.
guide.step1Note: The key value is shown only once at creation — store it somewhere safe.
guide.step2Body: The gateway speaks the OpenAI-compatible API: point your existing SDK or HTTP client at the gateway base URL and use your key.
guide.step2Note: Replace <gateway-host> with your environment's gateway address and <model-name> with a model from the catalog.
guide.step3Title: Pick a model from the catalog
guide.step3Body: The catalog shows what's available, what it costs, and when it changes. The catalog model name is exactly what you pass as the model parameter.
guide.step3Item1: Model Calendar — availability and schedule changes.
guide.step3Item2: Model Dashboard — pricing (input / output / cache read) and specs.
guide.step4Body: Your team page's Usage tab shows requests, tokens, and spend for every member.
guide.step4Item1: Input (cache rd) — input tokens, with the cache-read portion in parentheses; a bigger parenthetical means cheaper input.

settings.pageDescription: Manage default values applied across the entire portal
settings.apiKeyLimitsDescription: Default TPM/RPM limits applied when creating new API keys
settings.autoRegisterDescription: Automatically register new users on SSO login. The default team is assigned to all users; additional teams are assigned based on user ID prefix rules.
settings.defaultTeamHelp: Teams that all new users will be automatically added to. Use commas to specify multiple teams.
settings.extraTeamRulesHelp: If a user ID starts with the given prefix, additional teams will be assigned on top of the default team
settings.hideTeamsDescription: Manage teams hidden from regular users. Always visible to admins.
settings.cacheCatalogDescription: Manage Redis hash key suffixes for model cache storage (e.g. chat → GENERATIVE:AI:chat). Entries per suffix can be added in the cache settings section of the model detail sheet.
settings.suffixMinRequired: At least one suffix is required.

clusters.title: Kubernetes Clusters
clusters.description: Register K8s clusters for the portal to use. The kubeconfig is stored encrypted and never shown again. Which cluster a deployment/benchmark runs on is chosen in each menu.
clusters.name: Name
clusters.argocdNamespaceHint: Namespace where this cluster's ArgoCD watches Application resources (default: argocd).
clusters.kubeconfigEditPlaceholder: Paste a new kubeconfig to change it
clusters.kubeconfigEditHint: Leave empty to keep the existing kubeconfig.
clusters.defaultNfsHint: Mounted read-only into benchmark jobs that target a raw model on this cluster. Point the benchmark tokenizer under the mount path. Server, export path and mount path are required together.
clusters.nfsFieldsRequired: NFS server, export path and mount path must be set together.
clusters.testNeedsConfig: Enter a kubeconfig and context first.

llmd.title: llm-d stacks
llmd.description: Prefix-cache-aware serving stacks deployed via ArgoCD.
llmd.pageDescription: Deploy an Envoy + EPP standalone router (llm-d) in front of a running model via ArgoCD.
llmd.previewHint: The ArgoCD Application this stack will create.
llmd.previewEmpty: Fill in the form to preview the ArgoCD Application.
llmd.name: Name
llmd.targetModelHint: The router (EPP) is placed in front of this already-running model's pods.
llmd.endpointSelectorHint: Label selector for the model's pods (multiple labels = AND). Blank = llm-ops/model-name=<model>.
llmd.valuesOverrideHint: JSON object deep-merged over the generated chart values — set any chart option.
llmd.overrideInvalid: Values override must be a valid JSON object.
llmd.deleteConfirm: Delete stack "{name}"? ArgoCD will prune its workloads.
llmd.nameModelRequired: Name, model and served name are required
llmd.noResources: No resources reported yet (not synced or ArgoCD unreachable).
llmd.effectiveValuesHint: The full values the portal rendered and sent to ArgoCD (chart defaults + your override).
llmd.liveValuesHint: The values currently applied on the live ArgoCD Application.
llmd.valuesYamlHint: Full Helm values for the standalone chart. The image registry default is applied underneath if omitted.
llmd.liveErrorTitle: Couldn't read live state from ArgoCD
llmd.noResourcesError: Live state unavailable — see the warning above.

adminBenchmarks.pageDescription: Performance and accuracy benchmark runs against LiteLLM-registered models.
adminBenchmarks.compareMaxHint: You can compare up to {max} runs at once.
adminBenchmarks.pendingResult: Waiting for the runner to finish...
adminBenchmarks.confirmCancel: Cancel this benchmark run? The K8s Job will be deleted.

benchmarkForm.pageDescription: Spawn a benchmark Job against a LiteLLM-registered model. Parameters are stored alongside the result so the run can be reproduced later.
benchmarkForm.modelHint: Only models registered with the LiteLLM proxy are listed.
benchmarkForm.toolHint_vllm_serving: Performance: vllm-style concurrent load test (TTFT / TPOT / throughput).
benchmarkForm.toolHint_sglang_serving: Performance: sglang-style concurrent load test (TTFT / TPOT / throughput).
benchmarkForm.toolHint_lm_eval: Accuracy: lm-evaluation-harness against the LiteLLM proxy.
benchmarkForm.maxConcurrencyHint: Cap on in-flight requests (--max-concurrency). Blank = unlimited.
benchmarkForm.randomInputLenHint: Input tokens for the random dataset (--random-input-len).
benchmarkForm.seedHint: Random seed for the request stream. Vary it across runs for variance analysis.
benchmarkForm.randomRangeRatioHint: 0–1 spread around the input/output lengths (--random-range-ratio). Blank = fixed lengths.
benchmarkForm.goodputHint: Space-separated metric:ms SLOs; reports request throughput meeting all of them (--goodput).
benchmarkForm.genKwargsHint: Comma-separated generation controls passed to lm-eval (--gen_kwargs).
benchmarkForm.tokenizerHint: Blank uses the target model path. Alias targets may need a tokenizer path in air-gapped setups.
benchmarkForm.nfsOverrideHint: Mounts a model-weights NFS export into the benchmark job. Overrides the cluster default. Point the tokenizer under the mount path.
benchmarkForm.extraParamsHint: Merged on top of the named fields (overrides). Use this to pass any tool-specific option the form doesn't expose.
benchmarkForm.extraArgsHint: Appended to the command verbatim — for value-less flags a key/value object can't express, e.g. --disable-tqdm. Performance runs only.
benchmarkForm.errorExtrasNotObject: Extra params must be a JSON object.
benchmarkForm.tasksHint: Comma-separated lm-eval task names (e.g. mmlu, hellaswag).
benchmarkForm.numConcurrentHint: Concurrent calls into the LiteLLM proxy.
benchmarkForm.limitHint: Cap the # of evaluation samples for a quick run.
benchmarkForm.imageHint: Override only if you maintain a custom benchmark image.
benchmarkForm.apiKeyHint: Key the runner presents to the target. Overrides the auto-derived serving key / LiteLLM admin key. Use for auth-gated targets.
benchmarkForm.loadFromHint: Prefills the form from a past run's config. The API key is not restored — re-enter it if needed.
benchmarkForm.clusterHint: Which K8s cluster the benchmark Job runs on. Leave as default to use the portal's default cluster.
benchmarkForm.previewHint: The exact K8s resources that will be applied to the cluster with your current inputs. Sensitive values (API keys, etc.) are masked.
benchmarkForm.previewEmpty: Pick a target (serving deployment or model) to see the preview.
benchmarkForm.deploymentHint: Benchmark a portal-managed serving deployment directly (recommended).
benchmarkForm.modelHintDisabled: Ignored while a serving deployment is selected.
benchmarkForm.errorTargetRequired: Select a serving deployment or a model alias.
benchmarkForm.ephemeralHint: Clone the selected deployment into a throwaway serving, benchmark it, then tear it down. Requires a serving deployment as the template.
benchmarkForm.modeCloneHint: Spins up a copy of the serving, benchmarks it, then deletes it. Needs spare GPU capacity; RWO volumes may fail to attach while the live pod holds them.
benchmarkForm.modeDirectHint: Sends load to the running serving directly.
benchmarkForm.modeDirectUnavailable: Direct mode is only available for Ready portal deployments.

benchmarkCompare.pageDescription: Side-by-side metrics across {count} runs. Best and worst values are highlighted per row.
benchmarkCompare.needAtLeastTwo: Select at least two benchmarks from the list to compare.
benchmarkCompare.mixedKindsWarning: Selected runs mix performance and accuracy kinds — only shared fields (params, timestamps) are compared.
benchmarkCompare.perfLegend: Throughput rows: higher is better. Latency (TTFT / TPOT / total) rows: lower is better.
benchmarkCompare.accuracyLegend: Higher value is highlighted as best; this assumes the metric is a score (higher = better).
benchmarkCompare.servingLegend: Bold = differs across runs. Identical resource rows confirm a same-hardware comparison.

modelLimits.title: Per-model limits
modelLimits.editorHint: Empty = unlimited (∞). Clear all rows to remove the limit.
modelLimits.teamRetroHint: These per-model limits apply to all existing and new keys in this team (retroactive). To make one key different, set an override on that user's key.
